notes:
Round 1 medium finding (non-atomic rekey+sentinel in SettingsMigrationHelper.migrateIfNeeded) is resolved on commit e2b102b. Verified:

1. Single updateData call. SettingsMigrationHelper.kt now has exactly one dataStore.updateData { ... } block (lines 50-69). The trailing appSettings.markSettingsMigrated() second write is gone; .setSettingsKeysMigrated(true) is folded into the same builder chain at line 67 alongside clearPerModelOverrides() + putAllPerModelOverrides(remapped). A process kill between rekey and sentinel is no longer possible — DataStore persists the whole built AppSettings message atomically.

2. Regression test is sound. migration_writesRekeyAndSentinel_inSingleAtomicWrite injects a CountingDataStore (real DataStore<AppSettings> wrapper incrementing updateCount on every updateData, delegating I/O to the real store), calls resetCount() before helper.migrateIfNeeded(), then asserts updateCount == 1. The snapshot via rawDataStore.data.first() confirms BOTH settingsKeysMigrated == true AND the rekeyed key 'id/n' are present in the same snapshot. This directly guards the invariant that the medium finding asked for — a future split into two updateData calls would fail on the '1 atomic write' assertion and is thus reliably caught.

3. Low finding on AllowedModel.name defense-in-depth validation was explicitly deferred in round 1 as non-blocking for Task 2; the fix does not touch that boundary, and it remains non-blocking for this task.

4. No new issues introduced. The DeviceInfoCollector comment edit is pure documentation. The removed 'assertNull' test import is inert cleanup. The fold-in preserves prior builder semantics (the transform still clears the old map before putting the remapped one, and the sentinel flip is confined to the successful-build path — any exception inside the transform aborts the whole updateData without partial state).

No hardcoded secrets, no injection surface, no new auth/authorization regressions, no new logging gaps. OWASP Top 10 sweep clean for this diff. Task 2 security review: APPROVED.
